mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue support for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS value 700727.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month RSAFS changes are followed by nonpositive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, restrictive policy raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS value 5.33 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS-above-4.5% observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor demand and recession-risk repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE value 3.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or the downside hit rate is not higher than unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI is rising while the federal funds rate is above 5%, inflation persistence keeps policy restrictive and raises real-rate uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL value 311.054 and FEDFUNDS value 5.33
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate rising-CPI state has a 20-day S&P 500 mean return greater than or equal to the unconditional mean or a negative-signal hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts while the federal funds rate is elevated signals rate-sensitive demand contraction and weaker construction-linked earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST value 1331.0 and FEDFUNDS value 5.33
failure_condition: Reject if the joint falling-HOUST high-FEDFUNDS state is not associated with lower 60-day forward returns than other states in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in industrial production signals improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO value 102.3313 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends have a 5-day forward S&P 500 win rate below 50% or a rolling 3-year Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains with unemployment below 4% signal labor-market tightness that sustains wage and inflation pressure, forcing restrictive policy expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 0 and UNRATE < 4.0% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the tight-labor high-rate interaction produces 20-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional mean or fails to show a negative return hit rate above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth combined with a low unemployment rate signals broad expansion without immediate demand collapse, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 value 22668.986 as of t-5mo and UNRATE value 3.9
failure_condition: Reject if the positive-GDPC1 low-UNRATE state has 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean or positive return frequency below 50% in walk-forward evaluation.
